text:
REGIME — Geopolitical relief bid, risk-on surface masked by narrow breadth.

INDICES — VIX 25.8, elevated and rising. IWM +0.22%, IJR +0.33%, SPY +0.04%, QQQ +0.02%, DIA -0.19%; small caps outperformed large caps across the board. Most notable cross-asset move: VIXY +3.52%, flagging persistent hedging demand despite the equity bounce.

SECTORS — XLE +0.80%, XLK +0.48%, XLU +0.22%, XLV +0.20%, XLC +0.05%, XLF +0.00%, XLRE -0.10%, XLI -0.20%, XLB -0.28%, XLY -1.16%, XLP -1.69%. Top industries: WGMI +2.37%, XES +2.08%; bottom: XHB -2.23%, MSOS -8.56%.

THE 7s — Buildout +1.55%, Semi +1.43%, Old Metals +1.14%, Fab +0.79%, Energy +0.71%, Health +0.53%, Power +0.36%, Cloud +0.33%, Bank +0.23%, Industrial +0.07%, New Staples -0.06%, Insurance -0.10%, Freight -0.18%, Software -0.25%, Medtech -0.25%, Toll Road -0.35%, Defense -1.02%, New Metals -1.03%, Old Staples -1.90%, Housing -1.91%. Strongest ticker in Buildout 7 not specified; weakest in Housing 7 not specified from data.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 54.2, Neutral. Top movers: UNH +9.37% (Health 7, 2.5x vol), WGMI +2.37% (industries), MSOS -8.56% (industries). 20d momentum best sector: XLE +7.52%; worst: XLP -4.95%. Best basket: Energy +9.02%; worst: Software -9.85%. UNH's +9.37% surge on 2.5x volume likely reflects mean-reversion after prior-session healthcare sector pressure.

WATCH — UNH +9.37%, 2.5x vol spike warrants confirmation; one-day reversal of this magnitude at elevated VIX risks fade. MSOS -8.56%, extreme single-day drop deserves catalyst verification — trend break or liquidity event. XHB -2.23%, Housing 7 -1.91% both deteriorating on 20d basis (-6.53%); rate-sensitive divergence worth monitoring if IEF holds bid.
